text.title: STAR Response Examples
text.summary: Annotated strong, weak, and red-flag STAR responses showing what complete vs. incomplete evidence looks like.

example.note: Question: "Tell me about a time you had to manage a project that was falling behind." All four components present, specific context, quantified result.
example.code:
Situation: "Last year, our product launch was 6 weeks behind schedule due to unexpected technical debt. The CEO was briefing investors the following month."
Task: "As project lead, I needed to get us back on track without burning out the team or cutting critical features."
Action: "I did three things: First, I mapped all remaining work and identified the true critical path — 40% of planned features weren't needed for launch. Second, I negotiated with stakeholders to defer nice-to-haves. Third, I restructured the team into two-day sprints with daily standups to increase velocity."
Result: "We launched 1 week ahead of the revised schedule. The CEO's investor meeting went well, and we maintained 95% team retention during the crunch."

list.item: Specific context with real stakes (investor meeting deadline).
Clear personal responsibility ("As project lead, I").

section.title: Weak response requiring probing

example.note: Question: "Tell me about a time you resolved a conflict." No specific example, hypothetical framing.
example.code: "I'm good at resolving conflicts. I always try to understand both sides and find a win-win solution."

list.item: Hypothetical ("I always try"), not actual past behavior.

section.p: Prescribed probing: "Can you give me a specific example?" → "Walk me through what happened." → "What did YOU specifically do?"

example.note: Question: "Describe a time you made a mistake at work." Cannot give example, externalizes blame.
example.code: "I don't really make mistakes. I'm very careful and detail-oriented. If anything goes wrong, it's usually because of others not doing their part."

list.item: Lacks self-awareness — cannot identify a personal mistake.
Blames external factors for all problems.
Indicates poor accountability — high risk for blame-deflecting behavior on the team.
